capabilities: Ensure all internal dependencies are scoped to a private registry and verify package integrity to prevent dependency confusion attacks.

procedure.action: Initialize the package manager to scope internal namespaces to a private registry by setting the registry URL in the .npmrc or equivalent configuration file by loading environment-specific values from config file; assert all required keys are present via config_validate(); assert config_validation_error_count < 1.
Commit the lockfile to version control to ensure all dependencies are resolved to specific versions, preventing unauthorized updates from public registries.

Verify package integrity by checking the hash of downloaded packages against a known good hash using a tool like npm audit or yarn audit using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Observe for namespace squatting by periodically scanning public registries for packages with similar names to internal packages using a tool like npm-scope-check by polling the target metric at interval_ms < 60000; emit METRIC_ALERT if value exceeds threshold for 3 consecutive samples; assert alert_latency < 5000ms.

Finalize defense status by running a security audit tool that checks for dependency confusion vulnerabilities and generates a report of any findings using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Defend against dependency confusion attacks by scoping internal namespaces to a private registry, verifying package integrity, and monitoring for namespace squatting.
title: Dependency Confusion Attack Defense

failureConditions: Malicious packages are installed when internal namespaces are not scoped to a private registry, causing security breaches.
Unauthorized package updates occur when lockfiles are not committed, leading to potential dependency confusion attacks.
Package integrity is compromised when hashes are not verified, allowing tampered packages to be used in production.
metrics: all required keys are present via config_validate()
no vulnerabilities related to dependency confusion
all dependencies resolve to private registry
successConditions: hash(package) == known_good_hash for all internal packages
